intro: A California lawsuit claims that AI pricing algorithms banned under state law are still being used by gas stations and oil companies to inflate fuel prices, with the case naming firms including BP and 7-Eleven. Google is investing $75 million in film studio A24 through a DeepMind partnership focused on AI filmmaking research. Anthropic's engineering leader says Claude Code is reducing collaboration among programmers, while Microsoft is force-installing Copilot on Windows 11 for Microsoft 365 Business users outside Europe.

whitelist.title: Is AI Causing Higher Gas Prices? California Lawsuit Says It Is
whitelist.desc: The lawsuit cites a bill signed into law last year banning the use of common pricing algorithms.

whitelist.title: Google invests $75 million in A24 as DeepMind launches AI filmmaking research partnership
whitelist.desc: Google is putting roughly $75M into A24, the indie studio behind Backrooms, as part of a new research partnership with its DeepMind AI lab.

whitelist.title: Anthropic's engineering leader says Claude Code is making programmers lonelier
